name: create_pod
description: Create a new modular portable database pod (SQLite + metadata + embeddings). Use this when user wants to create a new pod for storing notes, documents, and embeddings. For listing existing pods, use list_pods instead.

pod_type.description: Type of pod: scholar (academic/research), health (medical records), shared (collaborative), projects (project-based)

name: list_pods
description: List all existing data pods. Use this when user asks to see what pods they have or wants to list all pods. For creating a new pod, use create_pod instead.

name: add_content
description: Add a note or content to an existing pod. Use this when user wants to add a note, memo, or content to a pod. For ingesting documents from files, use ingest_documents instead. For semantic search, use semantic_search instead.

pod.description: Name of the pod to add content to

name: query_pod
description: Query a pod with text search or SQL. Use this when user wants to search notes in a pod. For semantic search with embeddings, use semantic_search instead. For adding content, use add_content instead.

pod.description: Name of the pod to query

text.description: Text to search for in notes (simple keyword search)

name: export_pod
description: Export a pod as a portable zip file. Use this when user wants to export, pack, or backup a pod. The export includes SQLite database, metadata, and embeddings.

pod.description: Name of the pod to export

output.description: Output path for the zip file (optional, defaults to pod name)

name: ingest_documents
description: Ingest documents from a folder into a pod with automatic embedding generation. Supports PDF, TXT, MD, DOCX, PNG, JPG files. Uses file hash to skip duplicates. For adding individual notes, use add_content instead. For semantic search, use semantic_search instead.

pod.description: Name of the pod to ingest documents into

folder.description: Path to the folder containing documents to ingest

name: semantic_search
description: Perform semantic search using embeddings to find relevant content in a pod. Uses cosine similarity to rank results by meaning rather than keywords. Requires sentence-transformers to be installed. For basic text search, use query_pod instead.

pod.description: Name of the pod to search

top_k.description: Number of top results to return

name: list_documents
description: List all documents ingested in a pod. Use this when user wants to see what documents have been added to a pod. For semantic search, use semantic_search instead.

pod.description: Name of the pod to list documents from
